habitat.text: North-west America, but now a common weed in many counties in Britain.

keynotes.text: Cladonia is one of a numerous genus of lecidineous lichens. It grows abundantly in the woods and hedges and is a common species; it has no odour; taste sweetish and mucilagenous.

indications.text: Expectorant, a valuable medicine in whooping cough.

dosing.text: 2 OZ. of the plant decocted and mixed with honey makes a good expectorant and a safe medicine for children's coughs.

generalities.text: Cladonia rangiferina . The badge of the Clan McKenzie. Makes excellent food for reindeer. C. sanguinea . In Brazil is rubbed down with sugar and water and applied in the thrush of infants. [ Top ]
